answer:
Ecwid API requests are authorized using OAuth 2.0. You must provide an access token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your HTTP request [1][2]. Passing tokens via query parameters is disabled [2].

For custom, single-store applications, you can skip the OAuth flow and use the pre-generated access tokens found in your app dashboard [1][3]. For external or public apps, you must exchange a temporary code for an access token via a POST request to https://my.ecwid.com/api/oauth/token [4][5].

Example request:

```http
GET /api/v3/{store_id}/profile HTTP/1.1
Host: app.ecwid.com
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}
```
